questions.question: Explain the concept of a drainage basin.
questions.answer: A drainage basin is the area drained by a river and its tributaries where all water flows into the same river system.

questions.question: Explain the role of water divides in river systems.
questions.answer: Water divides separate drainage basins and determine the direction in which rivers flow.

questions.question: Explain the erosional work of Himalayan rivers.
questions.answer: Himalayan rivers erode mountainous regions intensely, forming gorges, valleys and carrying large quantities of sediment.

questions.question: Explain the formation of the Sundarban delta.
questions.answer: The Sundarban delta is formed by deposition of sediments brought by the Ganga and Brahmaputra rivers near the Bay of Bengal.

questions.question: Explain the braided channel of the Brahmaputra river.
questions.answer: Heavy sediment deposition causes the river to split into many channels forming braided streams.

questions.question: Explain why peninsular rivers have smaller drainage basins.
questions.answer: Because they originate from plateaus and have shorter courses compared to Himalayan rivers.

questions.question: Explain the significance of rift valley rivers.
questions.answer: Rift valley rivers flow through valleys formed by tectonic faulting.

questions.question: Explain why Narmada creates many scenic locations.
questions.answer: Because it flows through deep gorges and rocky valleys such as the Marble Rocks near Jabalpur.

questions.question: Explain the formation of oxbow lakes.
questions.answer: Oxbow lakes form when river meanders are cut off from the main river channel.

questions.question: Explain the formation of lagoons.
questions.answer: Lagoons form when sand bars or spits separate part of the sea from the mainland.

questions.question: Explain the importance of lakes in regulating climate.
questions.answer: Lakes moderate the climate by maintaining humidity and temperature in surrounding areas.

questions.question: Explain how lakes support tourism.
questions.answer: Lakes attract tourists for activities such as boating, recreation and scenic beauty.

questions.question: Explain the role of rivers in India's economy.
questions.answer: Rivers support agriculture, irrigation, transportation and hydroelectric power generation.

questions.question: Explain why rivers attracted early human settlements.
questions.answer: Because they provided water, fertile land and transportation routes.

questions.question: Explain the impact of river pollution on human life.
questions.answer: Polluted rivers contaminate drinking water and spread diseases.

questions.question: Explain the need for river conservation.
questions.answer: River conservation ensures sustainable use of freshwater resources and protects ecosystems.

questions.question: Explain the purpose of the Ganga Action Plan.
questions.answer: It was launched to reduce pollution and improve the water quality of the Ganga river.

questions.question: Explain the objectives of the National River Conservation Plan.
questions.answer: Its objective is to improve river water quality by controlling pollution.

questions.question: Explain the importance of clean river water.
questions.answer: Clean rivers provide safe drinking water and maintain aquatic ecosystems.

questions.question: Explain the effect of industrialisation on river pollution.
questions.answer: Industrialisation increases discharge of chemicals and waste into rivers.

questions.question: Explain why delta regions are densely populated.
questions.answer: Because delta regions have fertile soil suitable for agriculture.

questions.question: Explain how rivers help in transportation.
questions.answer: Rivers provide natural waterways for moving goods and passengers.

questions.question: Explain the importance of river valleys for agriculture.
questions.answer: River valleys provide fertile alluvial soil and water for irrigation.

questions.question: Explain why freshwater resources must be managed carefully.
questions.answer: Because freshwater is limited and essential for life and economic activities.

questions.question: Explain the importance of rivers in sustaining ecosystems.
questions.answer: Rivers support biodiversity and maintain ecological balance.
